name: 6p22_2_HIST1_histone_cluster
description: 6p22.1-p22.2 HIST1 histone gene cluster — the largest of three histone clusters in human (HIST1 here, HIST2 on 1q21, HIST3 on 1q42). Contains ~55 replication-dependent histone genes (H1, H2A, H2B, H3, H4 paralogs) over ~2 Mb. Each subtype has 6-15 near-identical paralogs (>97% identity). Read mapping to a specific H2A or H4 copy is essentially impossible without long reads or PSV-aware callers.

architecture.internal_structure: ~55 histone genes including 12x H2A, 17x H2B, 14x H3, 15x H4 paralogs; arranged in mixed-orientation tandem array; paralog identity often >99% at protein level, 90-99% at nucleotide level; no introns (replication-dependent histones).

related_diseases: rare somatic mutations in cancer (e.g. H3F3A H3.3 K27M)
